---
title: "Architecture"
---

## Design Principles

- **Agent runs outside** вЂ” containers are exec environments, not agent hosts
- **Sleep infinity** вЂ” containers have no daemon, just `sleep infinity` as PID 1
- **Pool for speed** вЂ” pre-warmed containers eliminate cold start
- **Volumes for persistence** вЂ” survive container restarts, enable release/resume
- **Bind mounts for zero-copy** вЂ” dedicated mode skips tar-based file transfer

## Sandbox Lifecycle

```
POST /sandboxes
  в”‚
  в”њв”Ђв”Ђ bind_mounts empty? в”Ђв”Ђв–¶ Spot: claim from pool, start
  в”‚
  в””в”Ђв”Ђ bind_mounts present? в”Ђв”Ђв–¶ Dedicated: create volume + container, start
  в”‚
  в–ј
Running в—Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ resume (new container, same volume + mounts)
  в”‚       в”‚
  в”‚       в”‚
  в”њв”Ђв”Ђ exec в”Ђв”Ђв–¶ command runs via docker exec
  в”‚       в”‚
  в”њв”Ђв”Ђ idle timeout в”Ђв”Ђв–¶ Timeout (terminal, volume kept for TTL)
  в”‚       в”‚
  в”њв”Ђв”Ђ container crash в”Ђв”Ђв–¶ Defunct (resumable)
  в”‚       в”‚
  в”њв”Ђв”Ђ DELETE /sandboxes/{id} в”Ђв”Ђв–¶ Completed (volume kept, resumable)
  в”‚
  в””в”Ђв”Ђ DELETE /sandboxes/{id}/volume в”Ђв”Ђв–¶ Destroyed (everything removed)
```

## State Machine

| State | Description | Resumable | Exec-able | Volume |
|-------|-------------|-----------|-----------|--------|
| `pending` | Just created | вЂ” | no | exists |
| `starting` | Container starting | вЂ” | no | exists |
| `running` | Ready for exec | вЂ” | yes | exists |
| `defunct` | Container died (OOM, crash) | yes | no | exists |
| `completed` | Released by client | yes | no | exists |
| `failed` | Setup/creation failed | вЂ” | no | exists |
| `timeout` | Idle/lifetime expired | yes | no | exists |

Terminal states (`completed`, `failed`, `timeout`) have volumes cleaned up by the reaper after `volume_ttl_secs`. Defunct sandboxes are also cleaned up after the TTL if not resumed.

## Spot vs Dedicated

| | Spot | Dedicated |
|---|---|---|
| **Source** | Pre-warmed pool | Created on-demand |
| **Startup** | ~instant (container start only) | ~1-2s (create + start) |
| **File I/O** | Tar API (`GET/PUT /files/`) | Bind mount (zero-copy) |
| **Use case** | Generic exec tasks | Project-specific work, host file access |
| **Pool** | Uses pool, replenished automatically | Bypasses pool |

Both modes get a persistent volume at `/home/sandbox` for caches, tools, and state.

## Exec Backpressure

Each sandbox has a permit pool sized to `max_concurrent_execs` (default 4, configurable per tier). REST and WebSocket execs acquire a permit before running. When all permits are in use, additional requests get `429 Too Many Requests`.

WebSocket sessions hold their permit for the entire connection lifetime. Permits are released automatically via RAII when the exec completes or the connection drops.

## Background Tasks

Den runs three background tasks:

1. **Reaper** вЂ” sweeps every `interval_secs`, stops idle/expired/crashed containers, cleans up volumes past TTL
2. **Replenisher** вЂ” tops up the warm pool every `replenish_interval_secs`
3. **HTTP server** вЂ” Axum with graceful shutdown on Ctrl-C

All tasks observe a shared `watch::channel` for coordinated shutdown.

## Security Layers

- **File API:** path traversal prevention вЂ” all paths resolved against `/home/sandbox`
- **Bind mounts:** blocked prefixes (`/proc`, `/sys`, `/dev`, `/etc`, `/root`, `/var/run/docker*`), blocked target (`/home/sandbox`)
- **Proxy:** SSRF protection вЂ” only Docker bridge IPs allowed (172.16-31.x.x, 192.168.x.x)
- **Exec:** 300s max timeout, 100MB body limit
- **Containers:** `cap_drop: ALL`, `no-new-privileges`, configurable memory/CPU/PID limits
